references.text:
[Al63] Altman, E., On a problem of P. Erd\H{o}s. Amer. Math. Monthly (1963), 148-157.
 
 [Er75f] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, On some problems of elementary and combinatorial geometry. Ann. Mat. Pura Appl. (4) (1975), 99-108.

remarks.text: For the similar problem in $\mathbb{R}^2$ there are always at least $n/2$ distances, as proved by Altman \cite{Al63} (see [93]). In \cite{Er75f} Erd\H{o}s claims that Altman proved that the vertices determine $\gg n$ many distinct distances, but gives no reference.

statement.text: Let $x_1,\ldots,x_n\in \mathbb{R}^3$ be the vertices of a convex polyhedron. Are there at least\[(1-o(1))\frac{n}{2}\]many distinct distances between the $x_i$?
